title: Eduversal Principal Appraisal Framework v1.0
description: The Eduversal Principal Appraisal Framework v1.0 is a level-aware summative appraisal system for partner-school principals. It comprises five frameworks weighted to produce a composite annual appraisal score (0-100, mapped to A-F predicate band). The item set visible to each principal is determined by their assigned appraisal level (Induction / Developing / Proficient / Lead). Inherits the schema family of the Eduversal Teacher Appraisal Framework v2.1 — same scoring scale, same predicate bands, same level_visibility pattern, same Cambridge alignment block — adapted to the leadership context. Operates alongside the Principal Operating Cadence (operational rhythm) and Principal Leadership Observation Rubric (formative observation) as the summative evaluation layer of the Eduversal Principal Development Programme (EPDP).

references: PSEL — Professional Standards for Educational Leaders (NPBEA, 2015) — cited where directly adapted
Robinson, V. — Student-Centered Leadership (2011) — leadership effect-size dimensions
Hallinger, P. — Leadership for Learning framework (2011)
Bryk, A. & Schneider, B. — Trust in Schools (2002)
Fullan, M. — The Moral Imperative of School Leadership (2003)
Eduversal Teacher Appraisal Framework v2.1 — schema family inherited

regulatory_alignment.title: Permendikbud 6/2018 — Penugasan Guru sebagai Kepala Sekolah
regulatory_alignment.relevance: Defines the three core duties of the principal: managerial, entrepreneurial development, supervision. F1, F2, F4 evidence draw from this.

regulatory_alignment.title: Permendiknas 13/2007 — Standar Kepala Sekolah
regulatory_alignment.relevance: Five competency dimensions: kepribadian, manajerial, kewirausahaan, supervisi, sosial — mapped across F1-F5.

regulatory_alignment.title: Permendikdasmen 7/2025 — Penugasan Kepala Sekolah
regulatory_alignment.relevance: Current regulation governing principal appointment, term length, allowance, rotation. Affects level_visibility (induction year mapping).

regulatory_alignment.title: Permendikdasmen 11/2025 — Beban Kerja
regulatory_alignment.relevance: 37 jam 30 menit weekly workload framework managed by the principal — F1 evidence.

regulatory_alignment.title: Permendikdasmen 13/2025 — Pendekatan Pembelajaran Mendalam
regulatory_alignment.relevance: Deep learning curriculum framework — F2 (instructional leadership) evidence.

regulatory_alignment.title: PP 19/2005 jo. 13/2015 — Standar Nasional Pendidikan
regulatory_alignment.relevance: Eight national education standards (8 SNP) — F1 + F4 evidence.

regulatory_alignment.title: Cambridge Handbook for Centres
regulatory_alignment.relevance: Centre-level expectations — F2 (instructional leadership for Cambridge) and F4 (outcomes).

weights._note: Sum of F1-F5 at 'developing' and 'proficient' levels = 1.00. F_LEAD adds 0.10 ONLY at 'lead' level, with proportional reduction of F1-F5 to maintain sum=1.00 (see level_visibility.weight_redistribution).

F2.when: Sept-Oct + Feb-Apr scheduled visit windows, paired with school_appraisals_v2 visit

level_visibility.note: Each item carries a 'visible_from' field specifying the minimum level at which it appears. Items visible from 'induction' appear for all levels; items visible from 'lead' appear only at lead level (and form the F_LEAD framework). Charter NN1: NO formal appraisal during induction year — appraisal level 'induction' is reserved for the End-of-Year-1 transition assessment, NOT a within-induction formative appraisal.

weight_redistribution.induction: F1: 0.50, F2: 0.50, F3-F5: 0 (suppressed). Used only at the End-of-Year-1 transition. Not a within-induction tool.
weight_redistribution.developing: F1: 0.20, F2: 0.45, F3: 0.15, F4: 0.15, F5: 0.05 (less experience → observation weighted higher)
weight_redistribution.lead: F1: 0.20, F2: 0.35, F3: 0.15, F4: 0.15, F5: 0.05, F_LEAD: 0.10 (F_LEAD added; F1+F2 reduced to maintain sum=1.00)

year_to_level_mapping.year_1: Year-1 principal is in induction overlay. NO formal appraisal (Charter NN1). At end of Year 1, induction completion certificate is issued (binary).
year_to_level_mapping.year_2: First formal appraisal at 'developing' level — end of full Year 2.
year_to_level_mapping.years_7_plus: lead (network mentoring + cross-school contribution required for F_LEAD)

frameworks.title: Operating Cadence Compliance
frameworks.description: Auto-aggregated evidence of consistent execution of the Principal Operating Cadence (83 tasks across P1-P8 foci). Measures the FREQUENCY and FIDELITY with which the principal runs the operational rhythm: daily huddles, weekly walkthroughs, monthly RKAS reviews, termly EDS cycles, annual visi-misi reviews, etc.
frameworks.items_note: Items pulled from weekly_progress and principal_progress collections via aggregation. No subjective scoring at this level — completion data + evidence-quality flags drive the score.

items.title: Compliance-tier task completion rate

rubric.1: <70% of compliance-tier tasks (43 tasks) completed with evidence on time

items.data_source: principal_progress aggregated against principal-operating-cadence.json compliance-tier tasks

items.title: Core-tier task completion rate
items.data_source: principal_progress aggregated against principal-operating-cadence.json core-tier tasks

rubric.1: Evidence missing or late on >25% of completed tasks
rubric.3: Evidence current on >=90% of tasks within 24h-48h windows
rubric.4: Evidence current on 100% of tasks; archive immaculate

frameworks.title: Leadership Observation
frameworks.description: Score derived from principal-observation-rubric.json visits. P1-P8 ratings (E=Established, D=Developing, N=Not Observed) are converted to numeric scores (E=4, D=2, N=skip-not-counted) and weighted-averaged. Minimum 2 scheduled visits per year (Sept-Oct + Feb-Apr); paired with school_appraisals_v2 visits to amortise the trip.

items.title: P1 — Strategic Vision & School Improvement Planning

frameworks.title: Stakeholder 360° Feedback
frameworks.description: Termly cycles collecting anonymous feedback from staff (weight 0.6 within F3) + parent komite representatives (0.25) + student council representatives (0.15). Minimum 5 respondents per cohort to anonymise. Items are 4-level Likert against the 8 leadership foci.
frameworks.anonymisation_protocol: Below-threshold cohorts are reported as 'insufficient response'. Individual responses never disclosed. Aggregate scores only.

frameworks.title: Outcome Indicators
frameworks.description: Auto-pulled from existing Eduversal AH dashboards. Measures the school's actual outcomes during the principal's year. NOT a measure of the principal's individual effort — a measure of the school's collective performance under their leadership.

items.title: EASE I + EASE II network position
items.data_source: EASE-Analytics dashboard, school's percentile position network-wide

rubric.2: Below median both cycles OR mixed
rubric.4: Top quartile both cycles OR clear year-on-year improvement

rubric.1: Significant decline in grade distribution OR Cambridge findings flagged

items.title: Stakeholder satisfaction surveys (Student/Staff/Parent)
items.data_source: Survey aggregate from StudentSatisfactionSurvey + StaffSatisfactionSurvey + ParentSatisfactionSurvey

frameworks.title: Self-Assessment & Reflection
frameworks.description: Termly self-assessment + annual reflection narrative. Low weight (0.05) but absence is disqualifying — a principal who has not engaged in structured self-reflection cannot be rated above 'developing' regardless of F1-F4 scores.

rubric.3: Both termly self-assessments completed with substantive narrative
rubric.4: Both completed with evidence-grounded narrative referencing specific cadence tasks and observation findings

rubric.3: Substantive narrative referencing specific cadence completion + observation findings
rubric.4: Reflective narrative integrates F1-F4 evidence with named next-year goals tied to leaders-track competency progression

frameworks.title: Network Leadership Contribution
frameworks.description: Lead-level only (Year 7+ principals). Adds 0.10 weight; items measure cross-school mentoring, network-level contributions, and principal-mentor activity. Requires active principal_mentor_endorsement certification (Charter NN3) for any task tied to mentoring partner principals.
frameworks.weight_note: 0.10 added at lead level only; F1+F2 reduced proportionally to maintain sum=1.00.

items.title: Active mentoring of partner-school principal mentee(s)
items.data_source: induction_assignments where mentorUid = principalUid AND status=active

rubric.3: One active mentoring assignment with consistent monthly coaching
rubric.4: Multiple assignments OR exceptional mentee outcomes documented

items.title: Network-level contribution (cross-school workshops, exemplar sharing)
items.data_source: Eduversal-network event participation log, exemplar contributions to network resource library

items.title: Cambridge Centre maturity contribution
items.applies_when: school is Cambridge Approved Centre at maturity-2 or above
items.data_source: Cambridge Centre evaluation, mentoring of pre-Centre partner schools

narrative_fields.label: Headline Strength
narrative_fields.description: The single most notable leadership strength evidenced this year. Specific, evidence-anchored.

narrative_fields.label: Priority Development Area
narrative_fields.description: The single most important area for growth in next year. Frames as opportunity, not deficit. Drives the leaders-track competency progression goal.

narrative_fields.label: Recommendation
narrative_fields.description: One of: 'continue' / 'development_plan' / 'non_renewal'. Charter NN5: this triplet (composite + band + recommendation) is the ONLY content shared with yayasan. Item-level scores are NEVER shared.

narrative_fields.label: Agreed Next-Year Goals
narrative_fields.description: 2-3 specific outcome-based goals for next year, tied to leaders-track competency advancement.

narrative_fields.label: Principal's Reflection
narrative_fields.description: Principal's own response to the appraisal output. Acknowledged ≠ agreed; right to dispute preserved.

narrative_fields.label: Confidential Note
narrative_fields.description: Eduversal HQ-internal only. Used sparingly — safeguarding, governance concerns, or escalation flags.

charter_compliance.NN1: No formal appraisal during induction year (Year 1). End-of-Year-1 produces induction completion certificate ONLY (binary). First formal appraisal end of Year 2 at 'developing' level.
charter_compliance.NN2: Principal's induction journal NEVER feeds this appraisal. Principal_journal collection has list:false rule; this framework explicitly excludes journal data from any F-item data_source.
charter_compliance.NN3: F_LEAD-1 mentoring evidence requires active principal_mentor_endorsement certification on the mentor's record before any mentee assignment can be created.
charter_compliance.NN5: Yayasan-facing report contains ONLY the recommendation triplet (compositeScore + predicateBand + recommendation) + agreed next-year goals. Item-level scores, P1-P8 observation notes, 360 individual responses are NEVER shared with yayasan. Foundation Representative sees the same triplet and co-signs.

coverage.note: Each framework item carries cambridge_school_leader_refs[] linking to the 25 CSL 2023 standards covered by competency_framework/leaders. Aggregate coverage matches the leaders track.

schema_lineage: Inherits the schema family of Eduversal Teacher Appraisal Framework v2.1 (appraisal-framework-v2.json). Same scoring_scale + predicate_bands + level_visibility + cambridge_alignment block shape. Adapted to leadership context. Where this file diverges from v2.1, it does so for principal-specific reasons (F2 data_source = principal_observations not direct observation; F4 = school outcomes aggregate; F_LEAD = network contribution layer).

provenance.regulatory_sources: Permendikbud 6/2018, Permendiknas 13/2007, Permendikdasmen 7/2025, 11/2025, 13/2025, PP 19/2005 jo. 13/2015, Permendiknas 63/2009 — verbatim in docs/research/permendiknas/
provenance.framework_sources: Cambridge School Leader Standards 2023, Cambridge Handbook for Centres, PSEL 2015, Robinson Student-Centered Leadership, Hallinger Leadership for Learning, Bryk & Schneider Trust in Schools, Fullan Moral Imperative
provenance.observation_rubric_source: Academic Hub/resources/principal-observation-rubric.json (8 foci P1-P8, formative E/D/N coding)
provenance.schema_lineage_source: Academic Hub/resources/appraisal-framework-v2.json (Eduversal Teacher Appraisal Framework v2.1)
provenance.operational_pattern_source: Adapted from EPDP Phase 2 design (docs/principal-development/HEYET-PROPOSAL.md §4.5) — closes the Principal summative appraisal gap identified in the cross-module audit on 2026-05-08.
